---
title: "Background"
---

This site records the aggregated metrics that [OpenAlex](https://openalex.org/)
publishes for each institution, and tracks how they change over time. It does
not recompute anything from individual works — every figure is read straight
from the institution's OpenAlex entity.

## Which institutions

The starting point is the [TU9](https://www.tu9.de/) alliance of nine German
universities of technology. In OpenAlex, an institution can have associated
institutions with a relationship of `parent`, `child`, or `related`. To avoid
double-counting the same output, the tracked set includes only institutions
that are **not linked to one another as `parent`/`child`** — a `related` link is
allowed. Concretely:

- the nine TU9 universities;
- two libraries that OpenAlex models as separate institutions with their own
  output — the [TIB](https://openalex.org/I2802635041) and the
  [SLUB Dresden](https://openalex.org/I3132420320);
- two university hospitals that OpenAlex links to their university only as
  `related`, not `child` — [Universitätsklinikum Aachen](https://openalex.org/I4210120689)
  and the [University Hospital Carl Gustav Carus](https://openalex.org/I4210162051)
  in Dresden.

A `child` institution is deliberately excluded when its parent is already
tracked (for example, TUM Klinikum, a `child` of the Technical University of
Munich). The full list, with OpenAlex and [ROR](https://ror.org/) IDs, is in
[`data-raw/institutions.csv`](https://github.com/slub/tu9-openalex/blob/main/data-raw/institutions.csv)
and can be edited to change the selection.

## What is tracked

For each institution and each snapshot, the pipeline stores:

- **works_count** — the number of works OpenAlex attributes to the institution;
- **cited_by_count** — total citations those works have received;
- **h-index**, **i10-index**, **2-year mean citedness** — from the entity's
  `summary_stats`;
- **works and citations by publication year** — from the entity's
  `counts_by_year`.

The complete OpenAlex entity is also archived as raw JSON per snapshot
(`data/snapshots/<slug>/<date>.json`), so any field not surfaced in the CSV
views can still be recovered later.

## How it is assembled

1. For every institution in `data-raw/institutions.csv`, the pipeline requests
   the OpenAlex institution entity
   ([`/institutions/<id>`](https://docs.openalex.org/api-entities/institutions)),
   using the polite pool and, where configured, a premium API key.
2. The tracked metrics are appended to `data/metrics.csv` as a new dated row
   per institution; re-running on the same day replaces that day's rows rather
   than duplicating them.
3. The yearly counts and per-institution views are rewritten, and `meta.json`
   is regenerated for the site.
4. A guard rail aborts the run if the total works count drops sharply against
   the previous snapshot, which usually signals a bad fetch rather than a real
   change.

## Caveats

OpenAlex coverage and attribution evolve continuously — affiliation strings are
disambiguated over time, and works are added or reassigned. A change between two
snapshots therefore reflects both real publishing activity and improvements in
OpenAlex's own data. Figures are best read as a consistent, reproducible series
rather than as an authoritative count.
